Title: Genome of Tripterygium wilfordii and identification of cytochrome P450 involved in triptolide biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antitumor, anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressive, and potential neuroactive pharmaceutical activities
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream oxidation step leading to dehydroabietic acid precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: miltiradienol; dehydroabietinol; miltiradienoic acid; dehydroabietic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes consecutive oxidations at C-18 of miltiradiene or abietatriene, converting the methyl group through alcohol/likely aldehyde to the carboxylic acid moiety of dehydroabietic acid-related products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor of downstream triptolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a pathway-limiting/control step for triptolide accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes triptolide accumulation; RNAi decreases triptolide and overexpression increases triptolide by ~70% in suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP728B70 was identified by multi-omics correlation, tissue specificity, and MeJA inducibility as a core triptolide-pathway CYP. 2. In engineered yeast, co-expression with TwCPR3 converted miltiradiene/abietatriene-derived substrates to alcohol and acid oxidation products including dehydroabietic acid. 3. RNAi reduced triptolide accumulation, while overexpression increased triptolide levels by ~70%, indicating functional importance and partial pathway control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene-to-metabolite network analysis; RNAi knockdown in T. wilfordii suspension cells; overexpression in suspension cells; heterologous co-expression in miltiradiene-producing yeast; substrate-feeding assays; GC-MS/UPLC metabolite profiling; NMR structural identification
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TwCPR3; Arabidopsis CPR in WAT11 yeast
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low overall transcript abundance, highest in root bark; induced by methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in root bark of T. wilfordii; inducible by methyl jasmonate in suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate induction of suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pPGK1; pTEF2 in yeast co-expression system; pH7WG2D overexpression vector in T. wilfordii suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and transcriptome sequencing deposited under NCBI BioProject PRJNA542587; metabolomics by UPLC/Q-TOF MS; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP728 family had no previously assigned biochemical function before this study; aldehyde intermediate was not directly detected but strongly inferred; catalytic activity resembles stepwise carboxylation seen in conifer CYP720B enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering to increase triptolide production in native cells or heterologous hosts; should be stacked with upstream diterpene synthases and CPR for pathway reconstruction and dehydroabietic acid production

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antitumor, anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressive, and potential neuroactive pharmaceutical activities
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to copalyl diphosphate as the first dedicated step toward abietane diterpene formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies committed precursor for triptolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports triptolide biosynthesis as an established upstream pathway gene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TwCPS1 is a previously identified diterpene synthase for triptolide biosynthesis and was used as a validated anchor in the gene-to-metabolite network. 2. It is co-expressed with triptolide/triptophenolide and highly expressed in root bark. 3. TwCPS1 is genomically adjacent to TwMS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously cloned/identified in prior studies; expression correlation with metabolites in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root bark; induced in elicited cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in root bark; associated with triptolide and triptophenolide in correlation network
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and transcriptome sequencing deposited under NCBI BioProject PRJNA542587; metabolomics by UPLC/Q-TOF MS; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as known pathway gene and benchmark for validating the gene-to-metabolite network; not functionally revalidated here
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream module for pathway reconstruction or stacking with TwMS and CYP728B70 in heterologous production systems

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antitumor, anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressive, and potential neuroactive pharmaceutical activities
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream cyclization step following CPP formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes copalyl diphosphate to form the abietane-type diene olefin miltiradiene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct diterpene scaffold precursor for downstream triptolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports triptolide biosynthesis as an established upstream pathway gene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TwMS is a previously identified diterpene synthase that forms miltiradiene, the scaffold precursor of triptolide. 2. It is strongly associated with triptolide/triptophenolide in the correlation network and highly expressed in root bark. 3. TwMS is adjacent to TwCPS1 in the genome.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously cloned/identified in prior studies; expression correlation with metabolites in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root bark; induced in elicited cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in root bark; associated with triptolide and triptophenolide in correlation network
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and transcriptome sequencing deposited under NCBI BioProject PRJNA542587; metabolomics by UPLC/Q-TOF MS; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Aromatization of miltiradiene to abietatriene is suggested to occur spontaneously
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream module for heterologous production of miltiradiene and for stacking with TwCPS1, CYP728B70, and CPR

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antitumor, anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressive, and potential neuroactive pharmaceutical activities
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalytic activity not identified in this paper, but RNAi indicates involvement in triptolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for downstream triptolide accumulation, exact step unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi knockdown decreased triptolide accumulation in suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TW011445.1 was selected from co-expression, tissue specificity, and inducibility analyses as a candidate triptolide-pathway CYP. 2. RNAi knockdown reduced both target transcript level and triptolide accumulation. 3. Enzymatic assays with available later intermediates did not identify its catalytic step.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown in T. wilfordii suspension cells; transcript analysis; triptolide metabolite quantification; heterologous assay with candidate intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly similar to TwCPS1/TwMS; high in root bark; differentially expressed after MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in root bark; inducible under methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and transcriptome sequencing deposited under NCBI BioProject PRJNA542587; metabolomics by UPLC/Q-TOF MS; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to a T. wilfordii-specific CYP (sub-)family according to Discussion; biochemical function unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for future pathway completion studies and possible gene stacking once catalytic role is resolved

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antitumor, anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressive, and potential neuroactive pharmaceutical activities
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalytic activity not identified in this paper, but RNAi indicates involvement in triptolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for downstream triptolide accumulation, exact step unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi knockdown decreased triptolide accumulation in suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TW012149.1 was prioritized from network correlation and expression analyses as a candidate CYP. 2. RNAi knockdown reduced transcript abundance and triptolide accumulation. 3. Direct catalytic activity toward tested intermediates was not observed.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown in T. wilfordii suspension cells; transcript analysis; triptolide metabolite quantification; heterologous assay with candidate intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly similar to TwCPS1/TwMS; high in root bark; differentially expressed after MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in root bark; inducible under methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and transcriptome sequencing deposited under NCBI BioProject PRJNA542587; metabolomics by UPLC/Q-TOF MS; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to a T. wilfordii-specific CYP (sub-)family according to Discussion; biochemical function unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for future pathway dissection and engineering after catalytic role is determined

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antitumor, anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressive, and potential neuroactive pharmaceutical activities
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalytic activity not identified in this paper, but RNAi indicates involvement in triptolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for downstream triptolide accumulation, exact step unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi knockdown decreased triptolide accumulation in suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TW006625.1 emerged as a candidate CYP from co-expression and differential expression analyses. 2. RNAi knockdown lowered transcript abundance and triptolide accumulation. 3. Its specific biochemical step remains unknown based on tested substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown in T. wilfordii suspension cells; transcript analysis; triptolide metabolite quantification; heterologous assay with candidate intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly similar to TwCPS1/TwMS; high in root bark; differentially expressed after MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in root bark; inducible under methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and transcriptome sequencing deposited under NCBI BioProject PRJNA542587; metabolomics by UPLC/Q-TOF MS; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to a T. wilfordii-specific CYP (sub-)family according to Discussion; biochemical function unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for pathway completion studies, but requires biochemical characterization before deployment
